project.name: Nookstead
project.concept: A cozy browser MMO where players build homesteads outside a living town populated by LLM-powered NPCs with memory, reflection, and autonomous planning.
project.genre: 2D Pixel Art MMO / Life Sim / Farming RPG
project.audience: Core (18-35, fans of Stardew Valley, Animal Crossing, Palia)
project.timeline: Long (14 months total; Phase 0 Prototype = 2 months)
project.unique_selling_point: First MMO where NPCs are full generative AI agents with memory, reflection, and autonomous planning -- not scripted characters but personalities that form opinions about each player and each other.

market_size.sam: $1.4B browser-accessible life sim and farming RPG segment

market_intelligence.target_audience_size: Estimated 45M players globally interested in cozy life sim games

direct_competitors: Palia (F2P MMO life sim, closest competitor)
Stardew Valley (single/co-op farming RPG, genre benchmark)
Animal Crossing: New Horizons (life sim, console-exclusive)
market_gaps: No browser-native MMO life sim exists -- the entire genre is app/console-locked
No life sim has AI NPCs with genuine memory and personality -- all use scripted dialogue trees
Palia is the only MMO life sim, and it has no AI-driven NPCs
No game combines farming RPG + persistent AI agents in a shared world
competitive_analysis.differentiation: Generative AI NPCs with memory and reflection create emergent, unique stories per server -- a fundamentally new category of social experience.

monetization.model: Free-to-Play with cosmetic shop + seasonal battle pass
monetization.pricing_strategy: Free base game; $5/season battle pass; $1-10 cosmetic items; $3-8 QoL expansions (extra inventory, land)

market_intelligence.go_no_go_decision: GO - Strong market gap with no browser-native AI-driven life sim MMO. USP is defensible and technically novel.
market_risks: LLM API costs may be prohibitive at scale without aggressive optimization
Palia or a major studio could add AI NPCs, reducing differentiation
Browser gaming perception as 'lesser' may limit adoption among core gamers
Generative AI regulatory landscape is evolving and uncertain
recommendations: Prioritize AI NPC quality in prototype -- this IS the USP, it must be compelling
Build for mobile-first web to maximize addressable audience
Establish cost-per-player benchmarks early in Phase 0
Consider influencer/streamer strategy -- AI NPCs create unique, shareable moments

telemetry_events.description: Player initiates or completes dialogue with NPC
Player buys or sells at market
Sampled player position for heatmap analysis

rendering.renderer: WebGL (Phaser.js AUTO mode with Canvas fallback)

milestones.name: M0.1 - Walking on Tiles
deliverables: Phaser.js integrated into Next.js app
Tiled map loaded and rendered (single town zone)
Player sprite moves with keyboard/touch input
Camera follows player with smooth scrolling
Collision detection with buildings and objects
success_criteria: Player can walk freely on a 64x64 tile map
60 FPS on desktop, 30 FPS on mobile

milestones.name: M0.2 - Multiplayer Sync
deliverables: Colyseus server running with game room
Player positions synchronized across clients
Player join/leave visible to all clients
Basic NPC entities moving on server-defined paths
Server-authoritative movement validation
success_criteria: 2+ players see each other moving in real-time
Server handles 10 concurrent players without degradation

milestones.name: M0.3 - NPC Talks
deliverables: Dialogue UI window (portrait + text + input)
Claude API integration for NPC responses
Seed persona system (JSON-based NPC identity)
One fully configured NPC (Marko the baker)
Hybrid input: quick replies + free text
success_criteria: Player can approach NPC and start conversation
NPC responds in character with < 3 second latency
Dialogue limited to 10 turns (5 per side)
NPC personality is consistent across conversations

milestones.name: M0.4 - NPC Remembers
deliverables: Memory stream storage (PostgreSQL)
Memory retrieval (recency + importance + relevance)
Importance scoring via LLM
Conversation context includes retrieved memories
Basic reflection system (daily summaries)

metrics.performance_target: 60 FPS desktop, 30 FPS mobile, < 3s NPC response

risks.risk: LLM API costs exceed $0.03/player-hour budget at scale
risks.mitigation: Aggressive caching, agent tiering (Full/Simplified/Background), dialogue limits (10 turns), batched reflections, Claude Haiku for routine tasks

risks.risk: NPC response quality degrades (hallucinations, breaking character)
risks.mitigation: Strict system prompts with seed persona, post-processing filters, A/B testing of prompts, moderation of player free-text input

risks.risk: WebSocket performance issues with 100 concurrent players
risks.mitigation: Server-authoritative architecture, 10 tick/sec rate, LOD for distant entities, lazy zone loading (chunks), load testing in Phase 0

risks.risk: Phaser.js + Next.js integration creates performance or build issues
risks.mitigation: Dynamic import of Phaser (no SSR), dedicated canvas component, early integration testing in M0.1

risks.risk: Player toxicity in free-text NPC dialogue
risks.mitigation: Input moderation filter before LLM, NPC agents ignore toxic content, player report system, auto-ban for repeated violations

risks.risk: Browser gaming perception limits adoption among core gamers
risks.mitigation: Focus on quality and unique AI experience; plan Electron wrapper for desktop distribution post-launch

project_notes.created_by: Game Studio Sub-Agents v1.0 - Master Orchestrator
project_notes.asset_pack: LimeZu - Modern Interiors, Modern Exteriors, Modern Farm, Modern User Interface
project_notes.notes: Phase 0 focuses on proving the core USP: a player walks through a town and talks to an AI NPC who remembers previous conversations. All other systems (farming, economy, quests) are deferred to Phase 1.
